steps.title: Calculate the retail price
steps.content:
The retail price is 270% of the wholesale price. Convert 270% to a decimal multiplier: 270% = 2.70. Multiply the wholesale price by this factor:
$$2.70 \times 5.00 = 13.50$$
The retail price is \$13.50.

steps.title: Apply the 70% discount to find the final price
steps.content:
When something is 70% off, the customer pays the remaining percentage, not the discount amount. Remaining percentage = 100% − 70% = 30%. So multiply the retail price by 0.30:
$$0.30 \times 13.50 = 4.05$$
The discounted price is \$4.05.
